cards.text:
Little House
* Lived comfortably in a small house.
* Perceived the world as small, round, and resembling a pale blue shell.

Little Nest
* Resided in a cozy nest under the care of the mother.
* Believed the world consisted of straw.

First Exploration
* Left the nest to explore.
* Discovered the world appeared to be made of leaves.

Beyond the Tree
* Ventured further than the familiar surroundings of the nest.
* Realization of being fit for adult responsibilities.

World Perception
* At different stages, the world seems made of differing materials: shell, straw, leaves.
* Each stage brings a new understanding and realization.

Expanding Horizons
* Initial perception of a small, contained world changes with exploration.
* Complexity of the world increases with each new experience.

Understanding Limitations
* Recognized the limitations of one's understanding.
* Neighbours also share the uncertainty of the world’s composition.

Pale Blue Shell
* A symbolic representation of the small, enclosed universe seen from a home.
* Contrast with the vastness and complexity encountered later.

Symbolism of Straw
* Straw represents simplicity and safety provided by the mother.
* Nesting symbolizes protection and care in the early stages of life.

A Journey of Growth
* An evolution from living in small and protected environments to embarking on a quest of understanding and responsibility.
* Transition from home and nest to exploration and labour.
